name: How can I check if a general contractor is legit and properly licensed in California?
headline: How can I check if a general contractor is legit and properly licensed in California?

mainEntity.name: How can I check if a general contractor is legit and properly licensed in California?
mainEntity.text: How can I check if a general contractor is legit and properly licensed in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best way to check if a general contractor is legit in California is to start with their license number. Every licensed contractor should give it to you right away. You can look it up on the California State License Board (CSLB) website to confirm it’s active, what type of work they’re licensed for, and whether there are complaints or disciplinary actions. You should also ask for proof of general liability insurance and, if they have workers, workers’ compensation insurance. Ask their insurance agent to email you a certificate made out to your name and address so you know it’s real and current. Next, check reviews and references. Read recent Yelp, Google, or neighborhood group reviews, paying attention to comments about communication, staying on budget, and handling problems. Then ask the contractor for at least three recent local clients you can call. When you talk to those homeowners, ask what went wrong and how the contractor handled it. Common red flags include: only taking cash; asking for a very large deposit; not wanting to use a written contract; being vague about who will be on site; or refusing to pull permits. If something feels off, get another bid. There are many contractors in the Pacifica and San Bruno area, so you do not need to ignore your gut just to keep a schedule.
